title: The Bell of Brightwater
lesson: Bravery grows when we listen, learn, and help others together.
prompts: In a small cottage home at dawn -- a wooden table, a stone hearth with a low fire, a round window looking out over a little village, soft golden light -- the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} fastens a woven cloak at one shoulder and lifts a small basket of bread from the table, looking calm and content at the start of an ordinary day. {IMAGE_STYLE}. Exactly one child in the frame, and no other people.

In a medieval village square at dawn -- timber cottages, a stone well, muddy paths, pale golden light -- the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} steps into the square holding the bread basket against their chest and turns toward the villagers, with a startled, worried expression. {ADVENTURE_ELDER_MARA} clasps the child's free hand in both of hers and points toward the chapel bell rope, looking frightened and pleading. {ADVENTURE_BLACKSMITH_TOMAS} slumps against the stone well, pressing a cloth to his forehead with a weary expression. {IMAGE_STYLE}. Exactly one child and two adults in the frame, and no other people.

Inside a medieval healer's cottage in the morning -- hanging herbs, wooden shelves of clay jars, a small hearth, warm amber light -- the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} points at an open herb book on the table, with a determined expression. {ADVENTURE_HEALER_ALDEN} holds a clay cup and traces a drawing in the book with one finger, looking grave. Make the interaction read physically: both faces and hands aim toward that one open book. {IMAGE_STYLE}. Exactly one child and one adult in the frame, and no other people.

Exactly two children and one adult in the frame, and no other people.

Exactly one child and one adult in the frame, and no other people.

Exactly one child in the frame, and no other people.

In an ancient forest in late morning -- mossy stones, twisted oak roots, ferns, shafts of green light -- the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} reaches one hand toward the tiny silver leaf plant while using the other hand to steady the herb book, with an amazed expression. A small fox made of warm golden light stands on a mossy stone and points its nose toward the plant, looking watchful. {IMAGE_STYLE}. Exactly one child in the frame, and no other people.

Exactly one child and two adults in the frame, and no other people.

gists: In a cozy cottage home at dawn, the child gets ready for an ordinary morning in their home village, showing where they come from before any trouble begins.
In the village square, Elder Mara takes the child's hand and pleads for help as the chapel bell warns of a spreading fever and the blacksmith sickens by the well, drawing the child into the rescue.
Inside the healer's cottage, the child points to an herb book while the healer explains that a rare silver leaf may cure the sickness.
Inside the healer's cottage, the child comforts a sick village child while the healer prepares simple herbs that are not strong enough yet.
At the village gate, the child prepares to leave for the forest while the gate guard opens the way with caution.
In the ancient forest, the child searches near a fallen log and discovers a tiny silver leaf plant among the ferns.
In the ancient forest, the child reaches for the silver leaf as a magical golden fox quietly guides them to the right plant.
At the village gate, the child runs home carrying the wrapped silver leaf while the relieved guard opens the gate wider.
Inside the healer's cottage, the child, healer, and elder work together to grind the silver leaf and pour medicine into clay cups.
In the village square, the child gives medicine to the sick blacksmith while the elder helps, showing the village beginning to hope.
Inside the healer's cottage, the sick child sits up after drinking medicine while the child and healer react with relief.
In the village square, the child rings the chapel bell and is crowned with a wreath as the healed villagers celebrate, showing how the adventure made the child braver and part of the home they saved.
texts: At dawn in a snug cottage above Brightwater, {NAME} fastened a warm cloak and lifted a basket of bread. Through the round window, the little village was waking up. "Another peaceful morning," said {NAME} with a smile.

Down in the square, the chapel bell clanged. Elder Mara grabbed {NAME}'s hand. "The fever has spread -- please help us!" Blacksmith Tomas groaned by the well. "What can I do?" asked {NAME}. "Find Healer Alden," said Mara.
In the healer's cottage, Alden opened a dusty book. "Only silver leaf can cool this fever," he said. {NAME} pointed to the picture. "Then I will find it," said {NAME}.
Little Elin shivered on a straw bed. {NAME} pressed a cool cloth to her hand. "Will I get better?" whispered Elin. "We will try," said {NAME}. Alden nodded, worried but ready.
At the gate, Rowan lifted the heavy bar. "The forest is deep," warned Rowan. "I will be careful," said {NAME}, holding the herb book tight. "Then hurry," Rowan said.
Under twisted oaks, {NAME} searched beside mossy stones. Leaves rustled, and every fern looked mysterious. "Silver leaf, silver leaf," whispered {NAME}. "Please be hiding close by."
A golden fox stepped onto a stone, bright as candlelight. It tipped its nose toward a tiny plant. "Is that it?" asked {NAME}. The fox blinked. "Thank you," said {NAME}.
{NAME} raced back through the gate with the plant wrapped safely. "You found it!" called Rowan, pulling the gate wide. "The village still has time," panted {NAME}.
Alden crushed the silver leaf while Mara lined up cups. {NAME} poured carefully. "Slowly," said Alden. "No drops wasted," said Mara. "For everyone," said {NAME}.
In the square, {NAME} carried the first cup to Tomas. "Drink this," said {NAME}. "For Brightwater?" asked Tomas weakly. "For all of us," said Mara, steadying his hand.
By evening, Elin sat up and sipped. "My head feels cool," she whispered. {NAME} squeezed her hand. "You are getting better," said Alden. {NAME} finally smiled.

When the bell rang again, it rang for joy. Mara set a leaf wreath on {NAME}'s head. "You listened, learned, and helped us all," she said. Little Elin hugged {NAME}'s arm. Brightwater cheered -- and {NAME} stood a little braver than before, proud of the home they had saved.
